public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as grain measurer.
appearance: Svala Coalward carries the marks of grain measurer in industrial grit: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a grain measurer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of grain measurer in and around Harvest Circle, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Harvest Circle while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to work shortfalls is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Svala Coalward can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Svala Coalward can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Svala Coalward carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia. In Harvest Circle, this lands inside a public field already shaped by harvest stewards, quota clerks.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Harvest Circle: the part of Harvest Circle where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

first_impression: Svala Coalward presents as Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a grain measurer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Grain Measurer, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Svala Coalward refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Grain Measurer too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Svala Coalward can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Svala Coalward adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Svala Coalward is Known locally through day-to-day work as grain measurer. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Grain Measurer.
social_texture: Svala Coalward speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Harvest Circle, people read them as quota-aware, contribution-minded, and never fully off the clock.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to production; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but work shortfalls becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Svala touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Svala Coalward when the party needs a socially grounded face for Harvest Circle: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
